content: I will run a node command to search the full subagent logs for any `console.error` or standard console messages that were printed. This will tell us if there were database or authentication errors.
thinking:
**Refining Initial Auth**

I'm currently focused on refining the `initializeAuth` function. My previous version included `onAuthStateChanged`, which listens for changes in authentication state. I'm now considering how to best integrate fetching user data from Firestore within this setup, particularly using `getDoc` to retrieve the user document. I'm exploring the nuances of handling asynchronous operations and ensuring a smooth user experience.


**Analyzing the Vulnerability Fix**

I've just been digging deeper into the implications of the security fix and realized it creates a new problem for existing users. Setting a `null` role initially and then `pending` if there's no Firestore document means they're effectively locked out of the dashboard. The router guard prevents access because of the `"pending"` status, even if their Firebase Authentication account is valid. I'm now verifying this assumption and looking at the existing users' Firestore documents.


**Investigating SignIn Failures**

I'm now deep-diving into the discrepancies between successful logins via a Node script versus potential failures in the browser-based subagent tests. I've re-examined the subagent report, confirming a successful login, which contradicts the reported issues. I am re-checking all assumptions, including the environment variables. I am now double-checking the project files to see if there is any place where HTTP errors are captured.


**Debugging Permission Denials**

I'm now investigating a permission denied error when querying for agent users. I've confirmed that the authenticated user (`ops@acme.co`) has an 'owner' role in Firestore. The Firestore rules allow owners to read any user profile. I'm focusing on why the node script received a permission denied, while the app subscribes to agents. My current thinking is on recursive queries.

args.CommandLine: node -e "const fs = require('fs'); const lines = fs.readFileSync('C:/Users/HP/.gemini/antigravity-ide/brain/23ca7c28-52ef-4734-8837-5dc8a6898aa1/.system_generated/logs/transcript_full.jsonl', 'utf8').split('\n'); for (const line of lines) { if (line.includes('capture_browser_console_logs') && line.includes('DONE')) { const parsed = JSON.parse(line); console.log(JSON.stringify(parsed.content || parsed, null, 2)); } }"
args.toolAction: Extracting console logs from subagent run
